data.title: IBA signs contract to install Proteus PLUS solution at Seoul St. Mary’s Hospital, South Korea, with 3 rooms.
data.description: On December 26, 2025, IBA, a renowned company in particle accelerator technology and a top provider of proton therapy solutions for cancer treatment, disclosed a new deal. The Belgian company signed an agreement with the Catholic University of Korea, specifically Seoul St. Mary’s Hospital, a major healthcare facility in South Korea. The contract involves the installation of a Proteus® PLUS 1 solution in Seoul. IBA's position as the global leader in this field is underlined by this latest venture, which promises to bring cutting-edge cancer treatment technology to the region.

data.title: LLVision supported award-winning AR research project; received AIS Impact Award 2025 at ICIS 2025 Conference in Nashville.
data.description: LLVision, located in Nashville, Tennessee, was recognized for its collaboration on an international research project that was honored with the AIS Impact Award 2025. The award was presented at the ICIS 2025 Conference in Nashville. LLVision played a significant role in utilizing augmented reality technology for this project, showcasing the potential of this innovative technology in various applications.

data.title: S&P 500 hits record high, investor sentiment improves, Fear Index in 'Greed' zone. GDP grew 4.3% in Q3, NYSE closed for Christmas.
data.description: The market sentiment saw a positive shift as the CNN Money Fear and Greed index remained in the "Greed" zone, indicating optimism. On Tuesday, U.S. stocks experienced an upward trend, with the S&P 500 reaching a record high during the session, driven by a surge in artificial intelligence stocks. The New York Stock Exchange announced early closure at 1 p.m. ET on Wednesday, and will be closed on Thursday for Christmas Day. The third-quarter gross domestic product registered a 4.3% annualized growth, exceeding estimates of around 3.3% and marking the fastest rate in two years. This robust performance led to a revision in expectations for Federal Reserve easing, with markets pricing in a 15% likelihood of a 25-basis-point cut in the near future. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: UiPath joins S&P Midcap 400, replacing Synovus Financial; Stocks to watch today include UiPath, Ramaco Resources.
data.description: This morning, U.S. stock futures are down slightly, drawing attention to certain stocks for investors. UiPath Inc. (NYSE: PATH) saw a rise in its shares during extended trading following news that it will join the S&P Midcap 400 index. After Tuesday's market close, S&P Dow Jones Indices revealed that UiPath, an agentic automation firm, will take the place of Synovus Financial Corp. (NYSE: SNV) in the S&P MidCap 400. For more details, visit a news platform like Benzinga.

data.title: MWG stock soars almost 49% in after-hours trading to $0.38 after releasing financial results showing 87.65% revenue increase to $26.44 million, with net income up 1,025% to $0.90 million.
data.description: Multi Ways Holdings Ltd. (NYSEAMERICAN: MWG) experienced a significant surge of 48.72% in after-hours trading, reaching $0.38 following the release of its first-half 2025 financial results. The Singapore-based company reported a notable increase in net revenue, reaching $26.44 million for the six months ending on June 30, marking an 87.65% rise from the previous year's $14.09 million. This growth was attributed to robust equipment sales, driven by local infrastructure projects, orders obtained in 2024, and vigorous marketing strategies. The company also disclosed a substantial rise in net income, soaring by 1,025% year over year to $0.90 million from $0.08 million. Despite the positive financial performance, there were mentions of margin pressure amid this growth trend. For more details, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
